generated_sql: SELECT o.customer_id, c.company_name, SUM(od.unit_price * od.quantity * (1 - od.discount)) AS total_net_revenue FROM erp_core.orders AS o JOIN erp_core.order_details AS od ON o.order_id = od.order_id JOIN erp_core.customers AS c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY o.customer_id, c.company_name ORDER BY total_net_revenue DESC LIMIT 3

tasks.sub_question: Compute total net revenue per customer across all orders and return the top 3 customers ranked by total net revenue in descending order.

execution_plan.rationale: The question asks for the top 3 customers by total net revenue, which is a direct aggregate over operational facts best served by SQL.
assumptions: Net revenue is computed from order line totals (quantity * unit price net of discounts) in erp_core.
Customers are ranked descending by total net revenue.
